message:
fix(sync): skip --rebuild orphan cleanup when --rename-prefix is also active

With `--rename-prefix`, the import stage rewrites every JSONL issue
id from its source prefix into the configured target prefix before
inserting into the database. The JSONL file itself is not
rewritten; it stays with the source prefix on disk.

The `--rebuild` post-import "orphan cleanup" step then computed:
  orphan_ids = db_ids - jsonl_ids
and deleted every issue in that difference. But `db_ids` now
contains the POST-rename ids (e.g. "target-xre") while `jsonl_ids`
contains the PRE-rename ids (e.g. "source-001"). The two sets never
intersect, so every imported row was classified as an orphan and
the entire database got wiped immediately after being populated.
The user experience was a successful "imported 47 issues" log line
followed by an empty DB on the next `br list`.

Fix: short-circuit the orphan cleanup when `--rebuild` and
`--rename-prefix` are both set. The `reset_data_tables` call at
the start of the rebuild path already cleared the DB, so the
post-import DB contents mirror the JSONL (modulo the prefix rewrite)
and there is no legitimate orphan set to compute.

The log message in the else branch explains the skip so an operator
reading `br --log-level=debug sync` output understands why the
orphan pass did not run.

All other combinations behave unchanged:
  --rebuild alone                → orphan cleanup runs (as before)
  --rename-prefix alone          → no orphan cleanup (as before; not a rebuild)
  --rebuild + --rename-prefix    → orphan cleanup SKIPPED (this fix)
